fact: March 14 is exactly one week before the spring equinox in the Northern Hemisphere, when day and night become perfectly equal!
In Antarctica, March 14 marks the beginning of winter darkness - the sun starts disappearing for months at a time!
Tree sap runs fastest around this date in maple forests, which is why maple syrup makers get super busy right now!
March 14 falls on the 73rd day of the year, which means there are exactly 292 days left until New Year's Eve!
Hibernating animals like bears and groundhogs are starting to wake up and get really hungry around this time of year!
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around March 14 in most places, making mornings brighter and brighter!
In the Southern Hemisphere, March 14 is deep in autumn - leaves are changing colors and falling just like October in North America!
Zodiac fans know that March 14 babies are Pisces - the fish sign that's ruled by the planet Neptune!
Migrating birds are flying thousands of miles overhead right now, following invisible magnetic highways in the sky!
Earthworms start wiggling up to the surface around this date because the soil is finally warming up after winter!
The moon phase on March 14 changes every year, but it's always getting closer to the spring full moon that determines when Easter happens!
